name: 2008 Financial Crisis
description: Collapse of the housing market and global banking crisis.

sectors.name: Technology
sectors.description: Resilient balance sheets allowed for rapid post-crisis growth.

sectors.name: Banking
sectors.description: Systemic risk and toxic assets led to massive write-downs.

sectors.name: Real Estate
sectors.description: The epicenter of the crisis; values plummeted globally.

lessons.title: Technology Resilience
lessons.text: Companies with strong balance sheets and high margins recover faster than leveraged financial institutions.

lessons.title: Banking Fragility
lessons.text: High leverage and interconnectedness can turn a sector-specific issue into a systemic collapse.

lessons.title: Government Intervention
lessons.text: Unprecedented stimulus and low rates eventually floor the market, but the path is volatile.

months.headline: Subprime Lenders Begin Failing
months.description: New Century Financial stops accepting new loan applications as defaults rise.
months.impact: Banking sector shows first signs of stress.

lesson.title: Early Warning Signs
lesson.text: Market bubbles often show cracks in niche sectors before the broad market reacts.

months.headline: Liquidity Crisis Hits Europe
months.description: BNP Paribas freezes three funds due to subprime mortgage exposure.
months.impact: Global interbank lending markets freeze up.

months.headline: Bear Stearns Collapse
months.description: JPMorgan Chase acquires Bear Stearns in a Fed-backed fire sale.
months.impact: Confidence in investment banks evaporates.

lesson.title: Systemic Risk
lesson.text: Interconnected financial institutions can drag each other down in a liquidity spiral.

months.headline: Lehman Brothers Files for Bankruptcy
months.description: The 158-year-old firm collapses, triggering a global financial panic.
months.impact: Global stock markets enter a freefall.

months.headline: TARP Bailout Passed
months.description: US Congress passes the $700 billion Troubled Asset Relief Program.
months.impact: Government intervention begins to provide a safety net.

months.headline: Market Hits Generational Bottom
months.description: S&P 500 touches 666 as sentiment reaches extreme pessimism.
months.impact: The worst of the selling pressure finally exhausts itself.

lesson.title: Market Bottoms
lesson.text: Markets often bottom when the news is at its absolute worst.

months.headline: Recession Officially Ends
months.description: The NBER declares the end of the Great Recession as growth returns.
months.impact: A long, slow recovery begins for the global economy.

sectors.name: Technology
sectors.description: Valuations decoupled from reality before a massive correction.

sectors.name: Consumer Goods
sectors.description: Traditional businesses were ignored during the bubble but provided safety.

sectors.name: Energy
sectors.description: Deregulation and new trading models created rapid growth and risk.

lessons.title: Valuation Matters
lessons.text: Buying great companies at astronomical prices is a recipe for poor long-term returns.

lessons.title: The 'Eyeballs' Trap
lessons.text: Revenue and user growth without a path to profitability is unsustainable.

lessons.title: Sector Rotation
lessons.text: When one sector becomes a mania, capital often flees traditional, profitable businesses.

months.headline: Internet Stocks Surge in New Year
months.description: Investors pour billions into any company with a '.com' suffix as the new millennium approaches.
months.impact: Technology sector valuations reach record highs.

months.headline: The IPO Frenzy Continues
months.description: TheGlobe.com and other startups see 600% gains on their first day of trading.
months.impact: Speculative fever reaches a boiling point.

months.headline: Y2K Fears Grip Global Markets
months.description: Concerns over computer systems failing at midnight cause minor liquidity hoarding.
months.impact: Market remains resilient despite technical anxieties.

months.headline: NASDAQ Hits All-Time High
months.description: The tech-heavy index touches 5,048. Analysts predict the 'New Economy' is here to stay.
months.impact: The absolute peak of the Dot-Com bubble.

lesson.title: Irrational Exuberance
lesson.text: When everyone is convinced a market can only go up, the risk is at its highest.

months.headline: The Bubble Bursts
months.description: A massive sell-off in tech stocks begins as investors realize many firms have no path to profit.
months.impact: Billions in paper wealth evaporate in a single week.

months.headline: Terror Attacks Shut Down Wall Street
months.description: The 9/11 attacks cause the longest market closure since the Great Depression.
months.impact: Global uncertainty and fear lead to a deep market correction.

months.headline: Market Finally Finds a Floor
months.description: After a 78% decline in the NASDAQ, the tech sector finally stabilizes.
months.impact: The end of the Dot-Com bear market.

name: COVID Market Crash
description: Sudden global market collapse due to the pandemic.

sectors.name: Technology
sectors.description: Remote work and digital transformation accelerated by years.

sectors.name: Travel & Energy
sectors.description: Global travel restrictions and reduced industrial activity decimated revenues overnight.

lessons.title: Black Swan Speed
lessons.text: Modern markets react with incredible speed to global shocks.

lessons.title: Trend Acceleration
lessons.text: Crises often act as catalysts for existing technological trends.

lessons.title: Liquidity is King
lessons.text: Central bank intervention can decouple market performance from economic reality.

months.headline: Mysterious Virus Reported in China
months.description: A new coronavirus strain emerges in Wuhan. Markets remain largely unconcerned.
months.impact: Initial uncertainty in Asian markets.

months.headline: Global Markets Begin to Panic
months.description: Virus spreads to Italy and Iran. Supply chains show signs of major disruption.
months.impact: The start of the fastest 10% correction in history.

months.headline: The Great Lockdown Begins
months.description: WHO declares a pandemic. US and Europe shut down. Markets enter freefall.
months.impact: Extreme volatility; multiple circuit breakers triggered.

lesson.title: Systemic Shock
lesson.text: When the entire global economy stops simultaneously, historical models become useless.

months.headline: Fed Launches 'Infinite' Stimulus
months.description: The Federal Reserve pledges unlimited support to keep credit markets functioning.
months.impact: Markets begin a massive 'V-shaped' recovery despite bad data.

months.headline: Vaccine Breakthrough Announced
months.description: Pfizer and Moderna report high efficacy for their COVID-19 vaccines.
months.impact: Travel and 'reopening' stocks surge as hope returns.

months.headline: Inflation Starts to Heat Up
months.description: Supply chain snarls and high demand drive prices higher than expected.
months.impact: Concerns grow that 'transitory' inflation might be permanent.

name: 1991 Indian Liberalization
description: End of 'License Raj' and opening of the Indian economy.

sectors.name: Technology
sectors.description: IT services became India's primary export engine.

sectors.name: Banking
sectors.description: Private banks introduced technology and efficiency.

sectors.name: Automotive
sectors.description: Old-world manufacturers faced new competition from global entries.

lessons.title: Policy as Catalyst
lessons.text: Economic liberalization can unlock massive dormant value in an economy.

lessons.title: The Middle Class Engine
lessons.text: Opening markets creates a consumer class that drives decades of growth.

lessons.title: Global Integration
lessons.text: Aligning local industry with global standards creates world-class competitors.

months.headline: India Faces Foreign Exchange Crisis
months.description: Forex reserves drop to just $1.2 billion, barely enough for two weeks of imports.
months.impact: Economic survival is at stake; gold reserves are airlifted to London.

months.headline: The 'LPG' Budget Presented
months.description: Manmohan Singh announces Liberalization, Privatization, and Globalization.
months.impact: The 'License Raj' begins to crumble; markets react with cautious optimism.

months.headline: Harshad Mehta Scam Exposed
months.description: A massive stock market fraud involving bank receipts is uncovered.
months.impact: Markets crash as the scale of the manipulation is revealed.

lesson.title: Market Integrity
lesson.text: Rapid growth must be accompanied by strong regulatory oversight to prevent systemic fraud.

months.headline: NSE Begins Operations
months.description: The National Stock Exchange introduces electronic trading to India.
months.impact: Transparency and efficiency in trading improve significantly.

name: Oil Crisis 1973
description: OPEC oil embargo causing massive energy price spikes.

sectors.name: Energy
sectors.description: Producers benefited from record-high crude prices.

sectors.name: Automotive
sectors.description: Gas-guzzling models became unsellable overnight.

lessons.title: Supply Chain Geopolitics
lessons.text: Reliance on a single region for critical resources is a major strategic risk.

lessons.title: Stagflation Reality
lessons.text: High inflation combined with low growth is the most difficult environment for investors.

lessons.title: Efficiency Innovation
lessons.text: Resource shocks force industries to innovate for efficiency or perish.

months.headline: OPEC Announces Oil Embargo
months.description: Arab oil producers cut production and ban exports to the US and allies.
months.impact: Energy prices triple; gas lines form across the country.

months.headline: Energy Crisis Grips the West
months.description: Daylight savings time is extended and speed limits are lowered to save fuel.
months.impact: Industrial production slows; consumer sentiment plummets.

months.headline: Stock Market in Worst Slump Since 1929
months.description: The S&P 500 has lost nearly 50% of its value in two years.
months.impact: A generation of investors is scarred by the stagflationary crash.

lesson.title: The End of Cheap Energy
lesson.text: The era of unlimited low-cost energy ended, forcing a global shift in industrial strategy.

name: Asian Financial Crisis 1997
description: Currency devaluations and financial collapse in East Asia.

sectors.name: Manufacturing
sectors.description: Devalued currencies made exports highly competitive.

sectors.name: Banking
sectors.description: Dollar-denominated debt became unpayable.

lessons.title: Currency Risk
lessons.text: Borrowing in a foreign currency while earning in a local one is extremely dangerous.

lessons.title: Contagion Effect
lessons.text: Financial crises in one market can rapidly spread to seemingly unrelated ones.

lessons.title: Export Competitive
lessons.text: A weak currency can be a powerful tool for rebuilding an export-led economy.

months.headline: Thailand Devalues the Baht
months.description: The Thai government is forced to float the currency after failing to defend its peg.
months.impact: The 'Asian Tiger' economies face a sudden loss of confidence.

months.headline: Contagion Hits Hong Kong and Korea
months.description: Speculative attacks spread across the region; the Won and Rupiah plummet.
months.impact: Global emerging market funds see record outflows.

months.headline: Russia Defaults on Debt
months.description: The Russian government devalues the Ruble and suspends debt payments.
months.impact: The crisis goes global, threatening major Western hedge funds.

lesson.title: The Domino Effect
lesson.text: In a globalized world, a local currency crisis can trigger a worldwide liquidity event.

name: Global Inflation Surge 2022
description: Post-pandemic supply shocks and energy price spikes.

sectors.name: Energy
sectors.description: Geopolitical risk drove oil and gas prices to multi-year highs.

sectors.name: Technology
sectors.description: Rising rates hurt the valuation of high-growth tech stocks.

lessons.title: Rates vs Growth
lessons.text: When interest rates rise, the present value of future earnings drops, hitting tech hard.

lessons.title: Commodity Supercycle
lessons.text: Underinvestment in physical assets can lead to prolonged price spikes.

lessons.title: Pricing Power
lessons.text: In inflationary times, only companies that can pass costs to consumers survive.

months.headline: Conflict in Ukraine Shocks Energy Markets
months.description: Natural gas and oil prices spike as geopolitical tensions reach a breaking point.
months.impact: Global inflation expectations are revised sharply upward.

months.headline: US Inflation Hits 40-Year High
months.description: CPI reaches 9.1%, driven by energy, food, and shelter costs.
months.impact: The Federal Reserve signals a pivot to aggressive rate hikes.

months.headline: Fed Delivers Third Straight 75bps Hike
months.description: Powell warns of 'pain' ahead as the central bank fights to cool the economy.
months.impact: Tech stocks and bonds sell off in tandem.

lesson.title: The End of Easy Money
lesson.text: A decade of zero-interest rates ended abruptly, forcing a massive repricing of all global assets.

name: Post-war Economic Boom
description: The golden age of capitalism following WWII.

sectors.name: Manufacturing
sectors.description: US industry supplied the world's reconstruction.

sectors.name: Consumer Goods
sectors.description: The birth of the modern consumer culture.

lessons.title: Reconstruction Growth
lessons.text: Rebuilding after conflict creates immense demand for industrial capacity.

lessons.title: Demographic Tailwinds
lessons.text: The baby boom created a predictable, growing market for decades.

lessons.title: Global Leadership
lessons.text: Economic dominance often follows military and technological leadership.

months.headline: Soldiers Return to a Changing Economy
months.description: Millions of veterans re-enter the workforce as wartime production shifts to consumer goods.
months.impact: Initial unemployment spikes but consumer demand is pent-up.

months.headline: Marshall Plan Announced
months.description: US pledges billions to rebuild war-torn Europe, creating massive export demand.
months.impact: Industrial manufacturing enters a multi-year boom phase.

months.headline: The Suburban Revolution
months.description: Levittown and similar developments signal the start of a massive housing boom.
months.impact: Consumer goods and appliance sales skyrocket.

name: AI Technology Boom
description: The generative AI revolution and computing shift.

sectors.name: Technology
sectors.description: Compute demand outstripped supply for years.

sectors.name: Energy & Infrastructure
sectors.description: Data centers required massive power grid upgrades and cooling systems.

lessons.title: Platform Shifts
lessons.text: New computing platforms create massive wealth for hardware providers first.

lessons.title: Winner Takes Most
lessons.text: In high-tech, the first-mover advantage and scale create deep moats.

lessons.title: Infrastructure First
lessons.text: Before the software wins, the picks and shovels (chips/power) must be built.

months.headline: ChatGPT Reaches 100 Million Users
months.description: The AI chatbot becomes the fastest-growing consumer application in history.
months.impact: A global race for generative AI supremacy begins.

months.headline: Nvidia Forecast Shocks Wall Street
months.description: The chipmaker predicts a massive surge in data center revenue due to AI demand.
months.impact: Nvidia stock jumps 25% in a single day, leading a tech rally.

months.headline: AI Integration Reaches Critical Mass
months.description: Every major software firm announces 'Copilot' features powered by LLMs.
months.impact: Productivity expectations rise; tech valuations remain elevated.

name: Electric Vehicle Rise
description: The transition from internal combustion to electric power.

sectors.name: Automotive
sectors.description: Software-defined vehicles challenged 100 years of engineering.

sectors.name: Energy
sectors.description: Battery metals became the new oil.

lessons.title: Disruption Lag
lessons.text: Incumbents often ignore disruption until it's too late to maintain margins.

lessons.title: Vertical Integration
lessons.text: Controlling the battery supply chain was the key differentiator for winners.

lessons.title: Regulatory Wind
lessons.text: Government subsidies and mandates can force an entire industry to pivot.

months.headline: VW Dieselgate Scandal Breaks
months.description: Volkswagen is caught cheating on emissions tests, shattering trust in diesel.
months.impact: The shift toward electrification accelerates as regulators tighten rules.

months.headline: Tesla Model 3 Production Begins
months.description: Elon Musk warns of 'production hell' as the mass-market EV enters the line.
months.impact: High volatility in Tesla stock as manufacturing hurdles emerge.

months.headline: Global EV Sales Hit Record Highs
months.description: China and Europe lead a massive surge in electric car adoption.
months.impact: Lithium and battery metal prices begin to skyrocket.
